summary: The main claim is: To that end, I think it's worthwhile to explicitly list such capabilities, and define milestones which specifically exercise them.
This milestone would especially indicate progress on creativity / insight.

The main claim is: I think we should put significant weight on the possibility that our STEM-capable AGI will be doing these things using symbol manipulation like statistics and approximately-Bayesian updates, not neural nets.
Note that there are exceptions to things like model-parameter uncertainty being Knightian.
The main claim is: I conclude with a methodological coda: ChatGPT’s behavior must be described and analyzed on three levels: 1) The experiments exhibit surface level behavior.
The pattern of differences between the old and the new story indicates how ChatGPT maintains story coherence.
The main claim is: Total bounty capped at $600, first come first served.

The main claim is: I think knowing better the "hardness" of intelligence would better inform our position on takeoff dynamics.
I suspect that it is, I think there's a lot of empirical evidence for it.
The main claim is: Like the IDA model, this considers whether the research can succeed in time for HLMI.
The main claim is: | | Shorty: The key variables seem to be size and training time.
I didn’t say anything about why we might think size and training time are the key variables, or even what “key variables” means.
The main claim is: I think that creating a research journal dedicated to AI alignment would help with both problems.
Yet I believe that with enough thinking about these problems, there is a way to build a net positive journal for the field.
The main claim is: This is a linkpost for My own take: Cool, not super surprising given GPT-3 and Image GPT.
I look forward to seeing what a bigger version of this would do, so that we could get a sense of how much it improves with scale.
The main claim is: GPT-3 seems to be good at understanding the goals that are set in the pin item to the point that it tries to achieve them faster then the person who wrote the guide desires.
The main claim is: Among other things: an explosion of new neural net architectures with more innate structure than the old “connectionist” models.
Some people in psychology became interested in using them as a model for human learning.
